body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
- Infrastructure and some initial application of the skills for #561 
- I have then applied these and iterated upon them for improved behaviour. Happy in future PRs for greater iteration - in theory almost every application can be used to improve the skill

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Create Claude `settings.json` - it encourages users to use skills and subagents. It prevents Claude using non-read-type git commands.
- Build some skills
  - `skill-creator` - to build new skills. This was used to build the following from the wishlist:
    - `base-show` adds show and summary methods to structs
    - `docstrings` manages the docstring formatting and manages `docs/src/API`
    - `error-message-manager` manages the error messaging format, to ensure actionable, context-laden messages
- I then used these skills on the repository:
  - `base-show` was used to create show and summary methods for all structs. Includes both a compact and full show-type method (e.g. so that in arrays the compact 1-line show() method is used). unit tests are also written for this.
  - `docstrings` methods was used to overhaul the docstrings.
  -  `error-message-manager` was used to remove `@assert` and update all error messages, it also adds `test_throw` error messages tested in the unit tests. Any longer `>3 line` message is now put into a helper function `_throw_xyz` that are listed at the bottom of files.
- The `skill-creator` ensures that skills have a self-improvement request after every application. Skills were performed a couple of times, using this self-improvement to improve their performance. Also I had it add examples from the repo into skills to help with consistency.
  
## Examples: Current results from `base-show`
Improves vastly upon the spam of 100's of lines that used to happen for high dimensional fields
```
julia> ekiobj
EnsembleKalmanProcess
  process    : Inversion
  N_ens      : 50
  N_par      : 50
  n_iter     : 20
  scheduler  : DataMisfitController
  accelerator: NesterovAccelerator

julia> ekiobj.scheduler
DataMisfitController
  terminate_at: 1.0
  on_terminate: stop
  n_iterations: 1

# demo of the compact show methods .. (from different example)
julia> (ensemble_kalman_process, ensemble_kalman_process)
(EnsembleKalmanProcess (Inversion, N_ens=20, 6 iter), EnsembleKalmanProcess (Inversion, N_ens=20, 6 iter)) 

julia> get_u(ensemble_kalman_process, return_array=false)
7-element Vector{EnsembleKalmanProcesses.DataContainers.DataContainer{Float64}}:
 DataContainer (2×20)
 DataContainer (2×20)
 DataContainer (2×20)
 DataContainer (2×20)
 DataContainer (2×20)
 DataContainer (2×20)
 DataContainer (2×20)
```
```julia
julia> dist = constrained_gaussian("test", 0.3, 5, 0, Inf, repeats = 30)
ParameterDistribution with 1 entry
  'test': VectorOfParameterized (30 distributions) [30 constraints]


julia> dist.distribution
1-element Vector{VectorOfParameterized{Normal{Float64}}}:
 VectorOfParameterized
  n_distributions: 30
  eltype         : Normal{Float64}
```
## Example: Current results from `docstring`
Replaces/adds `DocstringExtensions` where needed
```julia 
"""
An ensemble transform Kalman inversion process.

$(TYPEDEF)

# Fields

$(TYPEDFIELDS)

# Constructors

$(METHODLIST)
"""
```
And 
```julia
"""
$(TYPEDSIGNATURES)

Construct a `Sampler` process from a `ParameterDistribution` prior.

The `sampler_type` keyword selects the update algorithm: `"aldi"` (default) uses the
affine-invariant Langevin dynamics update (`ALDI`); `"eks"` uses the ensemble Kalman
sampler (`EKS`).

# Arguments
- `prior`: parameter prior distribution used to extract the prior mean and covariance.
- `sampler_type`: one of `"aldi"` (default) or `"eks"`.
"""
```
and adds to docs/src/API
```julia
## Update Groups

` ` `@docs
UpdateGroup
get_u_group
get_g_group
get_group_id
update_group_consistency
create_update_groups
` ` `
```

## Example: Current results from `error-message-manager`
It now has a consistent error format, and uses inlined functions to retain readability
```julia
# _throw_xyz() messages
...
if !(eltype(epoch) <: Int)
    _throw_obs_epoch_bad_eltype(eltype(epoch), length(epoch))
end

# ... with context of where it was thrown if ambiguous
...
if !hasmethod(iterate, [typeof(x)]) # no way of iterating
        _throw_x_not_iterable(x; where = :transform_constrained_to_unconstrained)
end

# ... with context if within a loop
for (key, val) in pairs(group_identifiers)
    ...
    for pn in key_vec
        pi = param_indices[pn .== param_names]
        if length(pi) == 0
            _throw_ug_bad_param_name(pn, param_names, key, key_vec)
        end
        ...
    end
   ...    
end
```

with the throw function looking like:
```julia 
@noinline function _throw_obs_epoch_bad_eltype(T, len)
    throw(ArgumentError("""
Epoch vector must contain integer indices.

Expected:
    eltype(epoch) <: Int

Got:
    eltype(epoch) = $T
    length(epoch) = $len

Suggestion:
    Convert the epoch to a Vector{Int}, e.g. `Int.(epoch)`.
"""))
end
```
It also replaces assert statements, with proper errors
```julia
# Replaces: 
@assert(haskey(param_info, "constraint"))

# To:
haskey(param_info, "constraint") || _throw_toml_missing_required_key("constraint", collect(keys(param_info)))

...

@noinline function _throw_toml_missing_required_key(key_name, got_keys)
    throw(ArgumentError("""
Parameter info dict is missing the required $(repr(key_name)) key.

Got keys:
    $got_keys

Suggestion:
    Ensure the TOML entry for this parameter includes a `$key_name = ...` field.
"""))
end
```

<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Add some initial Claude settings and skills. Apply these across the repo.
